data.title: As a developer, a dedicated agent evaluates implementation proposals against quality dimensions
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create a dedicated dso:approach-decision-maker agent (opus) that evaluates implementation proposals against 5 quality dimensions and returns a selection with ADR-style rationale or a counter-proposal.
**Why**: An independent evaluator combats anchoring bias and brings a second perspective to approach selection, catching anti-patterns before task breakdown.
**Scope**:
- IN: Agent definition, evaluation prompt (5 dimensions: codebase alignment, blast radius, testability, simplicity, robustness), context hierarchy rules, anti-pattern detection, ADR-style output format, counter-proposal capability, agent registration, CLAUDE.md agent table entry
- OUT: Resolution loop logic (Story 3), eval fixtures (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a dso:approach-decision-maker agent definition exists with evaluation prompt covering 5 dimensions
  <- Satisfies: "A dedicated dso:approach-decision-maker agent (opus) evaluates proposals against five dimensions"
- When this story is complete, the agent respects context hierarchy: epic success criteria non-negotiable, story done definitions are acceptance criteria, story considerations are advisory
  <- Satisfies: "respecting context hierarchy"
- When this story is complete, the agent returns ADR-style rationale (Context, Decision, Consequences) on selection or a counter-proposal satisfying the same done definitions
  <- Satisfies: "returns either a selection with ADR-style rationale or a counter-proposal"
- When this story is complete, the agent flags approach-level anti-patterns (golden hammer, premature abstraction, cargo cult, resume-driven development, premature optimization, not-invented-here) when detected
  <- Satisfies: "flags approach-level anti-patterns"
- When this story is complete, the agent includes explicit nesting prohibition (Read/Grep/Glob allowed, Task dispatches prohibited)
- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Named-agent dispatch table includes dso:approach-decision-maker with model (opus), dispatched-by (/dso:implementation-plan)
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must set timeout: 600000 on dispatch; apply context-budget cap for large stories
- [Reliability] Nesting prohibition must be explicit in agent definition
- [Testing] Novel pattern with low familiarity — eval coverage critical
- [Maintainability] Proposal schema must match skill expectations — reference shared schema
